title: Jenkins Agent Offline Alerts
app: Splunk App for Jenkins, Jenkins API
spl:
index=cicd sourcetype="jenkins:computer" (offline="true" OR offline=true)
| lookup jenkins_agent_tier displayName OUTPUT tier
| where tier="production"
| table _time, displayName, offline, labels, numExecutors
| sort -_time
description: Operational paging when executors disappear‚Äîcomplements UC-12.2.10 with severity tiers and agent labels (prod vs dev).
value: Operational paging when executors disappear‚Äîcomplements UC-12.2.10 with severity tiers and agent labels (prod vs dev).
implementation: Tag agents in lookup. Page immediately for production-labeled offline agents. Auto-create incident if >30% of pool offline.
visualization: Table (offline prod agents), Single value (count), Status grid (agent √ó tier).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk App for Jenkins, Jenkins API.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `/computer/api/json` `offline=true`, agent heartbeat.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Tag agents in lookup. Page immediately for production-labeled offline agents. Auto-create incident if >30% of pool offline.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="jenkins:computer" (offline="true" OR offline=true)
| lookup jenkins_agent_tier displayName OUTPUT tier
| where tier="production"
| table _time, displayName, offline, labels, numExecutors
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Jenkins Agent Offline Alerts** ‚Äî Operational paging when executors disappear‚Äîcomplements UC-12.2.10 with severity tiers and agent labels (prod vs dev).

Documented **Data sources**: `/computer/api/json` `offline=true`, agent heartbeat. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk App for Jenkins, Jenkins API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cicd; **sourcetype**: jenkins:computer. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=cicd, sourcetype="jenkins:computer". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where tier="production"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Jenkins Agent Offline Alerts**): table _time, displayName, offline, labels, numExecutors
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with Jenkins build history. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (offline prod agents), Single value (count), Status grid (agent √ó tier).
grandmaExplanation: We watch Jenkins health and jobs so executors, agents, and failures do not stall delivery.
knownFalsePositives: Pipeline noise during blue/green cutovers, dependency cache cold-starts, or scheduled maintenance windows.
